summary: OpenAI announces GPT-6 with claimed 10x reasoning improvements, native memory across sessions, and enterprise agent orchestration built in.

comments.text: the memory feature quietly changes the data retention story. enterprise legal teams will read this closely.
benchmark table omits cost per token. 10x reasoning at what multiple of price?
every launch claims 10x. the eval suite is picked after the model is trained.
native orchestration means the agent-framework startups just became features.
we migrated to the last 'memory' beta and rolled back in two weeks. state sync across sessions is genuinely hard.
gpt-6 remembers everything. my prod incidents included.

summary: Microsoft claims a fault-tolerant quantum milestone with its Majorana chip, saying practical quantum advantage for chemistry workloads arrives within 24 months.

comments.text: the nature paper behind this has a corrections section longer than the abstract. worth reading before repeating the claim.
'within 24 months' has been the quantum industry's answer for a decade.
fault-tolerant at 8 qubits. chemistry workloads need thousands. the gap is the whole story.
real bet: lock in pharma R&D budgets now, deliver compute later. azure playbook again.
sat in a quantum vendor pitch last quarter. slide 3 was applications, slides 4-40 were caveats.
quantum advantage is always two years away. superposition of timelines.

summary: Reid Hoffman argues AI will create more jobs than it destroys, citing historical automation waves and a new study on augmentation vs replacement in knowledge work.

comments.text: the cited study measures task augmentation, not employment. those are different claims wearing the same hat.
previous waves took a generation to rebalance. the transition cost is the part optimists skip.
'history says jobs come back' assumes the displaced and the hired are the same people. they never are.
the interesting question isn't job count, it's wage distribution. augmentation concentrates value at the top of the skill curve.
my team is 30% smaller than 2024 and ships more. nobody was 'replaced by ai' on paper.
ai will create jobs. mostly for people writing posts about whether ai creates jobs.
